reasoning: Based on historical variations and current climate trends, the global temperature anomaly for October 2025 depends heavily on whether El Niño or La Niña conditions persist. While the secular warming trend of ~0.2°C/decade elevates the expected baseline, an expected transition to La Niña or neutral conditions by late 2025 would likely result in an anomaly somewhat below the extreme high seen in October 2023 (+1.34°C). I estimate the core expected range for October 2025 to be between 1.00°C and 1.25°C. Since the target bracket is very narrow (0.05°C wide, from 1.10°C to 1.14°C) and sits near the middle of the expected distribution, it captures roughly 15-20% of the probability mass. Therefore, I assign an 18% probability to this specific outcome.
